cards.explanation: Gusto kong = I want to. Uminom = drink. Ng kape = coffee.

cards.source_text: You just want water, nothing else.
cards.explanation: Na lang = just. Gusto ko na lang = I just want.

cards.source_text: Lola offers more food. You're full. What's the polite response?
options: Busog na po ako, Lola. Salamat po!
cards.explanation: 'Busog na po ako' is the polite Filipino way to decline food from an elder.

cards.source_text: You want to cook. Say it:
cards.explanation: Gusto kong magluto = I want to cook.

wrong_answer_tracking.behaviour: Wrong answers go to back of current session queue.

alam_mo_ba: You can now express desire (gusto), refusal (ayaw), and soften requests (na lang po). That covers most social situations at a Filipino gathering.
The kong/mong structure (ko + ng linker) is the same pattern used in 'nanay ko' (my mom) — the ng just merges with ko when a verb follows.
